title: Arena Edge Cases, Security & Documentation
goal: Harden the arena workflow against edge cases (all failures in a round, single model auto-complete, state drift), enforce credential safety for arena UI components, and update documentation to cover arena features.

in_scope: Handle case where all responses fail in a round — allow reset but not advancement
Handle case where only one model is active after inference — auto-mark arena complete
Handle case where user tries to proceed with zero winners — blocking validation
Prevent arena state drift when Streamlit reruns due to unrelated interactions
Verify API key is never displayed in arena UI components or error messages
Verify error messages from inference failures in arena context are user-friendly
Ensure execution path consistency: all model calls route through ModelRunner/run_inference abstraction
Update README.md with arena feature documentation: flags, workflow, limitations
out_of_scope: Retry logic or automatic recovery from transient errors

inputs: All arena module code from Parts 01-04
PRD Week 1 section 5.3 — Advanced features and edge cases
outputs: src/app.py — arena-specific error handling in UI
README.md — updated with arena feature documentation
dependencies: 1_arena_engine/Parts 01-04 — all arena features must be implemented
checklist: In arena UI, detect when all inference results have errors and display st.error with option to reset only
When all results fail, disable the Proceed button and show 'All responses failed. Reset arena to try again.'
After inference, if only one active model remains (others may have been pre-filtered), auto-complete arena
Verify the zero-winner validation in advance_round() produces a user-facing message (not stack trace)
Add guard against arena state drift: if selected deployments change, warn user or auto-reset arena
Audit all arena UI rendering to ensure no API key or credential values appear in any component
Audit arena error messages: inference failures should show friendly messages (from _friendly_inference_error)
Verify all arena inference calls use run_batch_inference() (consistent execution path)
Update README.md with section: Arena Elimination Mode — describing flags, workflow steps, and reset behavior
Document known arena limitations in README.md: session-only state, sequential execution, max 5 models
acceptance_criteria: If all responses fail in a round, user can reset arena but cannot advance
If user tries to proceed with zero winners, blocking validation message appears
If only one model remains after inference, arena auto-completes with winner banner
Arena state survives Streamlit reruns without unexpected resets
API key is never displayed in any arena UI component or failure message
Error messages avoid exposing internal stack traces or sensitive data
All model calls route through the run_batch_inference() abstraction
README.md documents arena feature flags, workflow, and limitations
review_focus: All edge case paths have been exercised (all fail, single model, zero selection)
Security audit confirms no credential leakage in arena paths
README documentation is accurate and matches actual implementation
Error messages are actionable and non-technical
State drift handling doesn't introduce regressions in the happy path
risks_or_notes: Streamlit rerun behavior is the primary source of state drift — use session state defensively
Edge case testing requires live Azure resource or well-designed mocks
README content should be validated by following steps on a fresh setup
Auto-completion on single model should clearly indicate it was determined by elimination, not inference quality
definition_of_done: All identified edge cases are handled with user-friendly behavior
No credential leakage in any arena code path
Manual testing confirms happy-path and all error-path workflows
App doesn't crash under any single-point-of-failure arena scenario
